text: Last class I joined the video call and shared my screen.
Today I want to write a clear follow-up email after the meeting.
Subject
Greeting
Attach
Draft
Reply
Forward
CC
Regards
Please find attached the supplier report.
I would be grateful if you could send your comments by Thursday.
I would be grateful if you could reply by Thursday.
Please find attached the agenda. Best regards, Percival.
Hi Karen, I wrote a draft of the follow-up email. The subject is Risk Review, Next Steps. Can you check it before I send it?
Of course. The greeting and the recap look great. Did you attach the supplier report?
Yes. I wrote: Please find attached the report. And I added the line I would be grateful if you could send your comments by Thursday.
Perfect. One small thing: please add Daniel in CC, so he can see it too. Then you are ready to send.
Done. I added Daniel in CC. Should I also forward it to my manager?
Yes, please forward it to her. The email is clear and short. Great work, Percival. You can send it now.
Subject: Risk Review, Next Steps.
Dear team, thank you for your time today.
Best regards, Percival.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. Great meeting today, thank you. Could you send a short follow-up email to the team this afternoon? Please keep it simple. Start with a clear subject line, something like Risk Review, Next Steps. Then a short greeting, and three lines: thank everyone, write a quick recap of what we decided, and list the two action items with their owners. Please attach the supplier report, and add Daniel as a recipient in copy. End with a polite closing, Best regards, and your name. If you have time, send me the draft first, and I will reply with any small changes before you send it. Thank you so much.

Okay, let me read my draft out loud before I send it. Subject: Risk Review, Next Steps. Greeting: Dear team. First line: Thank you all for your time in today's meeting. Second line: As a quick recap, we agreed to update the supplier list and to test the new control by Friday. Third line: Please find attached the supplier report for your reference. I would be grateful if you could send your comments by Thursday. Closing: Best regards, Percival. I will add Karen and Daniel as recipients, put Daniel in copy, attach the report, and then send it. I think this draft is clear and short. I will forward it to my manager too.

First, Percival writes a clear subject line for the email. Then, he adds a short greeting and thanks the team. After that, he writes a quick recap of what the meeting decided. Later, he lists the two action items and attaches the supplier report. Finally, he adds a polite closing, checks the draft, and sends the email.
